title: SACRAMENTO—Assemblymember James C. Ramos (D-San Bernardino) today commented on the upcoming California Native American Day, to be celebrated on Sept. 22:
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State University system has failed to repatriate nearly 700,000 Native American human remains and artifacts.",
    "California has more than 100 tribes and several that are unrecognized due to a history of broken treaties and maltreatment.",
    "Federally recognized tribes in California generate approximately $9 billion annually through casino revenues.",
    "Not all federally recognized tribes own casinos, and many non-recognized tribes struggle to ensure the security and wellbeing of their members.",
    "Governor Gavin Newsom issued an apology to California’s Native Americans for historical violence and other wrongs four years ago.",
    "The California Native American Legislative Caucus was created to remove obstacles to services and rights for Native Americans in the state.",
    "More than 25 bills addressing issues affecting Native Americans have been signed into law in California.",
    "AB 3099 aims to increase communication among law enforcement agencies to combat the crisis of Missing and Murdered Indigenous People.",
    "AB 873 assists tribal courts in protecting tribal foster youth.",
    "AB 855 establishes the first Native American paid holiday for California state and local court employees.",
    "AB 945 reinforces Native American rights to wear tribal regalia at high school graduation ceremonies.",
    "AB 923 encourages consultation on a government-to-government basis between the state and tribes.",
    "AB 1703 aims to improve Native American education by encouraging local schools to engage with tribes.",
    "AB 44 grants tribal governments and courts access to the California Law Enforcement Telecommunications System to enhance public safety on reservations.",
    "AB 2022 prohibits the use of a derogatory slur for female Native Americans as geographic names in California."
  ]
}
```
